title: Active Sonar — underwater acoustic imaging
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence template (fan-out artifact). Physics below is grounded in canonical remote-sensing literature for this principle.

E.description: Active Sonar — underwater acoustic imaging: active sonar pulse produces the measurement through a 4-node primitive DAG L.emit.acoustic_pulse -> S.scan.beam -> L.beamform -> int.temporal, with time-integrated exposure and additive Gaussian thermal/electronic noise. Recovery is posed as a linear inverse problem that inverts the forward operator to estimate the scene-side 2D acoustic reflectivity. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~12; calibration-level mismatch (sound_speed_profile_error, multipath, platform_roll) sets the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form imaging equation.
E.forward_model: y(t, theta) = sum_r reflectivity(r) * pulse(t - 2r/c(r)) + reverberation + n
E.world_state_x: Scene-side quantity of interest (reflectance / velocity / phase / concentration)
E.observation_y: Sensor output at declared resolution + noise

W.regime: Existence of the recovered 2D acoustic reflectivity is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by the declared priors. Stability is moderately conditioned (kappa_eff ~= 12); sound_speed_profile_error dominates the stability cliff; multipath and the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Additive gaussian thermal/electronic noise sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor, while mild Tikhonov or analytic inversion is sufficient at the nominal Omega point.

C.solver_class: linear-operator + convex optimisation [Delay-Sum-Beamform, MVDR] | linear-operator + deep neural prior [Sonar-Net]
C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: ||x_hat - x||_2 <= C1 * sigma_noise / sqrt(N_measurements). Mismatch-induced bias adds + C2 * |sound_speed_profile_error| + C3 * ||x - x_s||_1 (best s-sparse / low-rank approximation residual). Iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the MLE; epsilon_fn captures the monotone quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(H * W * log(...)) per iteration; learned variants: O(H W Z * F_theta_cost) per forward pass
